Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given an event log with 5 cases Need to identify which cases have significantly longer resolution times determine potential root causes and propose recommendations

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First compute total resolution time for each case from first event Receive Ticket to last event Close Ticket Timestamps are given Lets calculate durations

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 101 Receive 2024 03 01 08 00 Close 2024 03 01 10 15 Duration 2 hours 15 minutes 135 minutes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 102 Receive 2024 03 01 08 05 Close 2024 03 02 09 15 Thats 25h10m 1510 minutes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 103 Receive 2024 03 01 08 10 Close 2024 03 01 09 30 Duration 1 hour 20 minutes 80 minutes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 104 Receive 2024 03 01 08 20 Close 2024 03 02 08 30 24h10m 1450 minutes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 105 Receive 2024 03 01 08 25 Close 2024 03 03 09 30 49h5m 2945 minutes

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Clearly cases 102 104 105 are much longer than 101 and 103 101 and 103 are under 2 5 hours 102 and 104 are about 25 hours 105 is about 49 hours So cases 102 104 105 are significantly longer

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 101 Simple path Receive Triage Assign Level 1 Investigate Resolve Close No escalation All steps quick

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 103 Similar

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 102 Receive 8 05 Triage 8 30 Assign 9 00 then Escalate to Level 2 at 11 30 2 5 hours later then Investigate at 14 00 2 5 hours after escalation then Resolve next day 9 00 19 hours later Close 9 15 So there is a long gap between Assign and Escalate then between Escalate and Investigate then huge gap between Investigate and Resolve

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 104 Receive 8 20 Triage 9 00 Assign 9 30 then Investigate at 13 00 3 5 hours later then Resolve next day 8 00 19 hours later Close 8 30 No escalation but long gap between Assign and Investigate and then between Investigate and Resolve

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 105 Receive 8 25 Triage 8 35 Assign 9 00 Investigate 9 10 then Escalate at 10 00 then next day at 14 00 Investigate again after escalate then Resolve at 2024 03 03 09 00 then Close So there is a long gap from escalation to next investigation 28 hours then from investigation to resolution next day 19 hours

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Cases with escalations 102 and 105 have longer total times But case 104 also long without escalation

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Long waiting times between activities specifically between assignment and investigation and between investigation and resolution Also between escalation and next investigation

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Many gaps span overnight indicating work stops at end of day and resumes next day

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This suggests that the resolution step may not be performed immediately after investigation perhaps due to end of day or need for approval

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Escalating to a Level 2 agent seems to add waiting time Possibly Level 2 agents are not readily available or have queued tasks

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In case 104 there is a 3 5 hour gap between assignment and investigation So maybe resource contention

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Bottleneck in investigation Possibly Level 1 agents take time to start investigation after assignment

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Some tickets take longer for triage case 102 25 min case 104 40 min compared to others 5 to 10 min But these are minor relative to overall

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: In case 105 there are two Investigate Issue events one before escalation and one after That could indicate that the initial investigation was insufficient and after escalation a deeper investigation was needed doubling the time

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Potential root causes escalations introduce significant delays overnight gaps long idle times between assignment and investigation bottleneck in investigation triage and assignment delays and multiple investigations

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Implement service level agreements for each step to ensure timely responses

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Ensure that after investigation resolution is performed promptly perhaps by the same agent or by scheduling handoffs

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Address the availability of Level 2 agents perhaps have a dedicated escalation team with faster response times or use a rotation system

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Monitor queues and assign tasks to agents with available capacity to reduce idle times

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Consider automating triage or using a priority system to ensure that tickets that may require escalation are identified early and routed quickly

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: For overnight gaps consider a 24 7 support model or shift handoffs to reduce idle time between end of day and next day

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: For case 105 the double investigation suggests that initial investigation might be incomplete ensure that Level 1 agents have clear guidelines on when to escalate and what initial investigation to perform

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Thus answer Cases 102 104 105 have significantly longer times Root causes escalations long waiting times between steps especially overnight and possible resource constraints
